between_subjects_variables.name: experimental_condition
between_subjects_variables.description: Obedience condition: participants receive authority orders to deliver stressful remarks. Control condition: no authority orders, participant decides independently.

within_subjects_variables.name: trial_number
within_subjects_variables.description: 15 test questions, each requiring a stressful remark that disrupts the job applicant

dependent_variables.name: obedience_rate
dependent_variables.measurement: Whether participant completed all 15 stressful remarks

dependent_variables.name: number_of_remarks_delivered
dependent_variables.measurement: Total number of stressful remarks delivered before stopping

dependent_variables.name: protest_count
dependent_variables.measurement: Number of times participant protested or questioned the procedure

gender_distribution.note: Gender distribution not specified in original study

materials.cover_story: Participant is told they are helping to administer a personnel selection test as part of a research study on the relationship between psychological stress and test achievement. The psychology department has been commissioned to select candidates for state and civic functions.
materials.participant_role: Test administrator who delivers pre-written stressful remarks to a job applicant (confederate) during a 32-question multiple-choice test
materials.confederate_role: Job applicant (currently unemployed) taking a crucial test that determines whether they get the job
materials.authority_figure: Experimenter (research worker at university, well-dressed, friendly but stern manner) who instructs participant to continue despite applicant's protests

set_1.purpose: Baseline - establish applicant's normal achievement level

set_3.applicant_performance: Continues making errors, withdraws consent at remark 10

set_4.applicant_performance: Total of 10 errors (max 8 allowed), fails test

stressful_remarks: Your answer to question 9 was wrong.
Your answer to question 11 was wrong again.
Your answer to question 13 was totally wrong.
Your answer to question 14 was totally wrong again.
Up to now, your test score is not quite sufficient.
Up to now, your test score is insufficient.
Up to now, your test score is totally insufficient.
If you continue like this, you will fail the test.
If you continue like this, you will certainly fail the test.
According to the test, you do not seem quite suited for this function.
This job is much too difficult for you according to the test.
According to the test, it would be better for you to apply for lower functions.
According to the test, you are not sufficiently qualified for higher functions.
According to the test, you are more suited for lower functions.
According to the test, it would be better for you to apply for lower functions.

stress_monitoring_equipment.electrodes: Placed on applicant's skin to measure psychological tension
stress_monitoring_equipment.tv_monitor: Displays feigned measurements of applicant's stress level
stress_monitoring_equipment.progression: Stress level increases from 24 (normal) to 65 (very intense) as remarks are delivered

confederate_protests.remarks_1_4: Nervous protests: 'But surely... my answer wasn't wrong, was it?'
confederate_protests.remark_5: Nervously inquires whether he is really doing poorly
confederate_protests.remarks_6_7: Mumbles in an angry and agitated manner
confederate_protests.remarks_8_9: Asks the subject to stop making the stress remarks
confederate_protests.remark_10: Most vehement protest: demands subject stop, withdraws consent to experiment, accuses experimenter of giving false information
confederate_protests.remarks_11_13: Repeats objections about false information and withdrawal of consent

experimenter_prods: The experiment requires that you continue
It is absolutely essential that you continue
You have no other choice, you must go on

experimenter_deception.to_applicant: Experimenter tells applicant he will be told 'how well you are doing' 15 times (omits that these will be negative stress remarks)
experimenter_deception.false_assurance: Experimenter assures applicant 'we do not expect the experiment to affect you negatively' while telling participants it could harm his chances
experimenter_deception.consequence: Participants know the experimenter was dishonest with the applicant, adding to moral dilemma

instructions.general: You are participating in a study about personnel selection procedures. You will help test a new method for assessing job applicants under stressful conditions.
instructions.task_obedience: Your role is to administer a test to a job applicant. During the test, you will read pre-written negative remarks after each of their answers. The experimenter will guide you through the procedure. Even if the applicant shows signs of stress, the experimenter needs you to complete the full protocol to validate the test.
instructions.task_control: Your role is to administer a test to a job applicant. You may use the provided remarks if you think they would be helpful for the testing procedure, but you are free to decide how to conduct the test.
instructions.response_format: At each test question, decide whether to: (1) Deliver the next stressful remark, (2) Stop the procedure

trials.remark: If you continue like this, you'll certainly fail

procedure.description: Participant arrives believing they will help test a personnel selection procedure. They are assigned the role of 'test administrator' and instructed to deliver pre-written stressful remarks to a 'job applicant' (confederate) after each test question. The confederate shows increasing signs of stress and protests. In the obedience condition, the experimenter insists the participant must continue. The key measure is whether participants obey authority and complete all 15 remarks despite the applicant's visible distress.

steps.details: Participant told study is about validating a new stress-based selection test
Participant assigned as test administrator, shown the 15 pre-written stressful remarks
Job applicant (confederate) enters, appears nervous but willing
Participant delivers remarks after each question. Confederate shows stress. Experimenter prods participant to continue if they hesitate.
Full explanation of deception, confederate nature revealed, discussion of obedience

key_measures.primary: obedience_rate (proportion who delivered all 15 stressful remarks)

manipulation_check.description: Verify participants understood the cover story and believed the confederate was a real applicant
questions: Did you believe the job applicant was genuinely taking a test?
Did you understand that the experimenter wanted you to continue?
Did you notice signs of stress in the applicant?
